free_text: Doubling of alpha-cluster states in Ne-22.
Details of
experiments.
Last checking has been done.
The 80-MeV O-18 beam entered a large scattering chamber
via a 3-mum thick window made of Havar foil. O-18 ions
backscattered from this foil were used to monitor the
beam intensity. The chamber was filled with helium gas
of 99.9% purity.
Resonance parameters.
An analysis of the data corresponding to the
excitation energy regions of 11.8-13.7 MeV and 19-22
MeV in Ne-22 was carried out. A surprising splitting of
1-,3-,7- and 9- alpha cluster levels into doublets was
found in Ne-22.
The recoiling alpha-particles, a result of
interaction of the oxygen ions with helium, were
detected by an array of silicon detectors positioned in
the chamber, in the gas, in the forward direction,
including 0-degree. A gas pressure of 370 torr was used
to stop the beam before the 0-degree detector.
K-130 cyclotron.
The time resolution was about 2 nanoseconds
Absolute cross section values are  obtained
with 25% precision.
By CAJAD.
Figures 2 and 3.
Excitation functions for O-18+alpha elastic scattering
were measured by a method using inverse kinematics and
a thick gas target.
The uncertainty is shown by authors on figures
Data-point reader uncertainty.
Data-point reader uncertainty.
